text.title: Tool Card Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tool Card Template application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Tool card fits in one page (~70 lines markdown); deeper material lives in linked sub-docs.

rationale.source: Tool Card Template authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Long cards stop being read; agents and humans both bail past ~150 lines.

rule.statement: Description field is the prompt the model sees; covers when-to-call, when-not-to-call, and one worked example.

rationale.source: Tool Card Template authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Models route on the description; missing fields = mis-routes.

rule.statement: Card names a single accountable owner (handle / role with rotation).

rationale.source: Tool Card Template authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; future readers need a named human.

rule.statement: Card embeds or links ≥3 eval rows showing canonical calls + expected outputs.

rationale.source: Tool Card Template authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Without eval rows the card cannot be regression-tested when the schema evolves.

rationale.source: Tool Card Template authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Tool semantics drift; un-versioned cards mis-route agents on stale assumptions.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
